LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PER YVANOVA V. NEW CENTURY MORTGAGE (2016) 62 CAL. 4TH 919, THE BANK'S ARGUMENT THAT MR. ENG DOES NOT HAVE STANDING TO CHALLENGE THE ASSIGNMENT OF THE LOAN LACKS MERIT. PER JOLLEY V. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC (2013) 213 CAL. APP. 4TH 872, THE BANK'S ARGUMENT THAT THE COURT SHOULD TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE PPA AND THE INTERPRETATION AND MEANING ASCRIBED TO IT BY THE BANK AND FIND AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE BANK IS THE ACTUAL BENEFICIARY ALSO LACKS MERIT. SCOTT V. JP MORGAN CHASE (2013) 214 CAL. APP. 4TH 743 DOES NOT REQUIRE A DIFFERENT RESULT. MOREOVER, THIS ARGUMENT DOES NOT TAKE INTO ACCOUNT THAT MR. ENG HAS ALSO PLED THAT THE UNDERLYING LOAN WAS VOID AB INITIO. ALL OF THE BANK'S STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENTS LACK MERIT BECAUSE THE FACE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT CLEARLY AND AFFIRMATIVELY SHOW THAT ANY OF MR. ENG'S CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED. ON A DEMURRER, THE COURT IS REQUIRED TO ACCEPT THE ALLEGATIONS THAT CLAIMS MR. ENG DID NOT DISCOVER THE GROUNDS FOR HIS CLAIMS TILL FEBRUARY 2013 OR LATER AND THAT SOME OR ALL OF HIS CLAIMS DID NOT ACCRUE UNTIL HE WAS DISPOSSESSED. ALL OF THE CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING PURSUANT CRC 3.1312. THE COURT MAKES NO RULING AS TO RES JUDICATA AND COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. JUDGE: JUDGE KAHN. MOTION / Amended Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Peter L. Kurubes, Esq. (310-277-1040) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Martin Eng

Attorney, Chase Haslam of Bryan Cave LLP (415-675-3480) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A.

Attorney, Leticia C. Butler of McCarthy & Holthus LLP (619-685-4800) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party, Quality Loan Service Corporation

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S Amended Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to show that res judicata bars this case because CGC-09-487853 and this case do not involve the same cause of action. Plaintiff alleges new facts, e.g., the foreclosure sale that did not exist at the time the prior action was filed. (Planning & Conservation League v. Castaic Lake Water Agency (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 210, 227-228.) The court again rejects the statute of limitations argument and it was improper to raise the argument without citing any material change in the law. (CCP 438(g)(1).) Lastly, defendant fails to show that the litigation privilege bars the negligence and RICO causes of action because those claims are not limited to judicial/litigation communications. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order tracking the Court's ruling verbatim and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendant Jp Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Goli Mahdavi of Bryan Cave LLP telephone number 415-675-3448 for Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank.
Jospeh De Clue of De Clue Law Group, PC telephone number 949-596-7145 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Martin Eng.
Leticia Butler of McCarthy & Holthus, LLP telephone number 619-685-4800 appeared by telephone for Defendant Quality Servide Corp.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is overruled in its entirety. Per Yvanova v. New Century Mortgage (2016) 62 Cal. 4th 919, the Bank's argument that Mr. Eng does not have standing to challenge the assignment of the loan lacks merit. Per Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC (2013) 213 Cal. App. 4th 872, the Bank's argument that the court should take judicial notice of the PPA and the interpretation and meaning ascribed to it by the Bank and find as a matter of law that the Bank is the actual beneficiary also lacks merit. Scott v. JP Morgan Chase (2013) 214 Cal. App. 4th 743 does not require a different result. Moreover, this argument does not take into account that Mr. Eng has also pled that the underlying loan was void ab initio. All of the Bank's statute of limitations arguments lack merit because the face of the first amended complaint does not clearly and affirmatively show that any of Mr. Eng's claims are time-barred. On a demurrer, the court is required to accept the allegations that claims Mr. Eng did not discover the grounds for his claims till February 2013 or later and that some or all of his claims did not accrue until he was dispossessed. All of the claims are adequately pled. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling pursuant CRC 3.1312. The Court makes no ruling as to Res Judicata and collateral estoppel.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
